mission.title: gitea: Implement actions

steps.title: Understand the problem
steps.description: The target of this PR is to bring a basic implementation of "Actions", an internal CI/CD system of Gitea. That means even though it has been merged, the state of the feature is **EXPERIMENTAL**, and p

Start runner Clone the [act_runner](https://gitea.com/gitea/act_runner), and follow the [README](https://gitea.com/gitea/act_runner/src/branch/main/README.md) to start it. If all is well, you'll see a new runner has been added:

steps.title: Review the fix
steps.description: The fix was implemented in https://github.com/go-gitea/gitea/pull/21959. Review the changes to understand the solution.

steps.title: Verify the fix
steps.description: Confirm that the issue is resolved in your environment by testing the affected functionality.

codeSnippets: Start it. If all is well, you'll see the management page of runners: #### 2. Start runner Clone the [act_runner](https://gitea.com/gitea/act_runner), and follow the [README](https://gitea.com/gitea/act_runner/src/branch/main/README.md) to start it. If all is well, you'll see a new runner has been added:

Start it. If all is well, you'll see the management page of runners: #### 2. Start runner Clone the [act_runner](https://gitea.com/gitea/act_runner), and follow the [README](https://gitea.com/gitea/act_runner/src/branch/main/README.md) to start it. If all is well, you'll see a new runner has been added: #### 3. Enable actions for a repo Create a new repo or open an existing one, check the `Actions` checkbox in settings and submit. If all is well, you'll see a new tab "Actions": #### 4. Upload workflow files Upload some workflow files to `.gitea/workflows/xxx.yaml`, you can follow the [quickstart](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/quickstart) of GitHub Actions. Yes, Gitea Actions is compatible with GitHub Actions in most cases, you can use the same demo:

If all is well, you'll see a new run in `Actions` tab: #### 5. Check the logs of jobs Click a run and you'll see the logs: #### 6. Go on You can try more examples in [the documents](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions) of GitHub Actions, then you might find a lot of bugs. Come on, PRs are welcome. See also: [Feature Preview: Gitea Actions](https://blog.gitea.io/2022/12/feature-preview-gitea-actions/) Use `hex.EncodeToString(util.CryptoRandomBytes(20))` to replace `base.EncodeSha1(gouuid.New().String())`. Does this compete with systems like [woodpecker](https://github.com/woodpecker-ci/woodpecker)? I wonder if we could somehow combine efforts. Also, I would reconsider the name. Many people associate "bots" with "robots" which are user accounts to do automated things, e.g. chat bots, github bots, etc. > Does this compete with systems like [woodpecker](https://github.com/woodpecker-ci/woodpecker)? I wonder if we could somehow combine efforts. > > Also, I would reconsider the name. Many people associate "bots" with "robots" which are user accounts to do automated things, e.g. chat bots, github bots, etc. @silverwind There are some explanations in #13539, maybe that's a better place to discuss the design? 😊 Is "bots" the name of the whole CI/CD feature? If yes, then I think that's not a good name because bots are usually something different (machine controlled user accounts). It's not only CICD, that's why we don't name it Gitea CI. We have a name discuss in #13539, maybe automate is a better name. > Is "bots" the name of the whole CI/CD feature? If yes, then I think that's not a good name because bots are usually something different (machine controlled user accounts). @KN4CK3R Join [the naming party](https://github.com/go-gitea/gitea/issues/13539) or you'll be late. 😄 maybe not necessary to show the `long-time`, just show a time duration after the previous item is okay. or show nothing about time is also okay. ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25342410/206233313-f07df6ef-6ac1-484c-be37-92360c3e6cbf.png) > maybe not necessary to show the `long-time`, just show a time duration after the previous item is okay. or show nothing about time is also okay. You're right, I notice that both drone CI and GitHub don't show the timestamp, at least by default. I just removed it, and maybe we can provide an option later to choose "long time", "relative time" or none. suggest move summary to head , example: ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25342410/206452571-0da2ecfe-360f-4480-949d-470a56506200.png) and I think `%30` for left width is better: ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25342410/206450565-0007501c-2403-4f5d-891a-620136ff59ae.png) > suggest move summary to head ... > and I think `%30` for left width is better ... Done, and now it looks like: # [Codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/go-gitea/gitea/pull/21937?src=pr&el=h1&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github&utm_content=comment&utm_campaign=pr+comments&utm_term=go-gitea) Report > Merging [#21937](https://codecov.io/gh/go-gitea/gitea/pull/21937?src=pr&el=desc&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github&utm_content=comment&utm_campaign=pr+comments&utm_term=go-gitea) (dd319a9) into [main](https://codecov.io/gh/go-gitea/gitea/commit/cc1f8cbe96c195aab79761c48bc4ec0bff2b3431?el=desc&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github&utm_content=comment&utm_campaign=pr+comments&utm_term=go-gitea) (cc1f8cb) will **decrease** coverage by `1.02%`. > The diff coverage is `28.94%`. :mega: This organization is not using Codecov’s [GitHub App Integration](https://github.com/apps/codecov). We recommend you install it so Codecov can continue to function properly for your repositories. [Learn more](https://about.codecov.io/blog/codecov-is-updating-its-github-integration/?utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github&utm_content=comment&utm_campaign=pr+comments&utm_term=go-gitea)

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with gitea installed or the issue environment reproducible.
